data.title: Corvus Pharmaceuticals rises 5%, prices $175M stock offering at $22.15
Apple starts Lunar New Year discounts in China, up to 1,000 yuan
Asian equities mostly higher as Trump withdraws Greenland tariff threats
data.description:
Asia stock markets were mixed on Thursday, tracking a rally on Wall Street overnight as investors reacted positively to reduced tensions over U. S. demands for Greenland, as President Trump decided against imposing tariffs on Europe regarding the territory.

Gold fell more than 1% to around $4,780 per ounce on Thursday, easing from a fresh record hit in the previous session.

South Korea’s economy contracted by 0.3% QoQ in the three months to December 2025, reversing a 1.3% expansion in Q3 and missing expectations for a 0.1% increase. 

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 2.05% to above 53,400 while the broader Topix Index rose 0.9% to 3,622 on Thursday, snapping a five-day losing streak. The Japanese yen slipped toward 158.5 per dollar on Thursday, remaining under pressure amid a deteriorating fiscal outlook, while the Bank of Japan kicked off a two-day policy meeting. 

Japan’s trade surplus declined to JPY 105.7 billion in December 2025, down from JPY 120.3 billion a year earlier, missing expectations for a JPY 357 billion increase as imports grew faster than exports. Exports rose by 5.1% year-on-year to JPY 10,411.5 billion. Meanwhile, imports increased by 5.3% to JPY 10,305.8 billion.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.06% to above 4,120, while the Shenzhen Component slipped 0.1% to 12,245 on Thursday, as mainland stocks showed mixed performances amid an intensifying regulatory crackdown on trading activity, and the offshore yuan held steady around 6.95 per dollar on Thursday, after sliding in the previous session.

China’s urban youth unemployment rate for 16- to 24-year-olds, excluding students, fell to 16.5% in November 2025 from 16.9% in the previous month, marking the lowest level since June, according to data from the National Bureau of Statistics released on Thursday. 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.25% to 26,514 in early Thursday trading, reversing gains from the prior session as declines spread across most sectors.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.44% to 82,414 in Thursday morning trade, snapping a three-day losing streak

Australia (AS51) rose 0.75% to around 8,850 in early Thursday trade after a three-session losing streak. The Australian dollar strengthened to around $0.679 on Thursday, approaching a sixteen-month high.

Australia's unemployment rate decreased to 4.1% in December 2025, down from 4.3% in November, which was better than the expected 4.4%. Unemployment fell by 29,800 to 628,600.

Employment in Australia jumped 65,200, led by full-time hiring.

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher, recovering part of the previous session’s losses as markets welcomed President Trump’s remarks at the World Economic Forum ruling out the use of military force in efforts to acquire Greenland.

U.S. stock futures edged higher on Thursday as geopolitical and trade tensions between Washington and Europe eased: Dow +0.20%; S&P 500 +0.28%; Nasdaq +0.39%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: Goldman Sachs lifts 2026 gold forecast to $5,400 on strong private, central bank demand
data.description:
Goldman Sachs raised its end-2026 gold price forecast to $5,400 an ounce, citing stronger-than-expected central bank buying and sustained investor demand.

The bank increased its December 2026 gold price target by more than 10%, assuming that private investors who purchased gold as a hedge against macroeconomic risks will hold their positions through year-end, analysts Daan Struyven and Lina Thomas said in a Jan. 21 note.

"We assume private sector diversification buyers, whose purchases hedge global policy risks and have driven the upside surprise to our price forecast, don't liquidate their gold ‌holdings in ‍2026, effectively lifting the starting point ‍of our price forecast," the bank said ‌in a note dated Wednesday.

Goldman Sachs expects Western ETF holdings to rise as the U.S. Federal Reserve is likely to cut the funds rate by 50 basis points in 2026.

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) climbed to a peak of $4,887.82 per ounce on Wednesday, and has advanced more than 11% so far in ‌2026, extending a blistering rally that saw it jump 64% last year. BofA recently noted that the new world order is the spark for the new gold bull market.

data.title: RTX wins $380M navy contract; Lockheed gets $202M army award
data.description:
• Raytheon Co. of Tucson, Arizona, a business unit of RTX Corp. (RTX), was awarded a $380.8 million U.S. Navy contract modification, the Defense Department said on Wednesday.
  • Lockheed Martin (LMT) was awarded a $202.8 million contract modification to inspect, recertify, and repair PAC-3 missiles, returning them to serviceable condition, the statement read. The work will be performed in Grand Prairie and is expected to be completed by June 30, 2028.

data.title: Japan’s trade surplus tightens to JPY 105.7B as government stimulus sparks import growth surge
data.description:
Japan’s trade surplus declined to JPY 105.7 billion in December 2025, down from JPY 120.3 billion a year earlier, missing expectations for a JPY 357 billion increase as imports grew faster than exports.

Exports rose by 5.1% year-on-year to JPY 10,411.5 billion, hitting a record high but falling short of November’s growth rate and market forecasts due to ongoing U.S. tariffs on Japanese automakers.

Meanwhile, imports increased by 5.3% to JPY 10,305.8 billion, outpacing market predictions and marking the highest growth in eleven months. This increase was driven by strong domestic demand due to a major stimulus package introduced by the government.

The Nikkei 225 Index climbed 1.2% to above 53,400 while the broader Topix Index rose 0.9% to 3,622 on Thursday, snapping a five-day losing streak. The Japanese yen slipped toward 158.5 per dollar on Thursday, remaining under pressure amid a deteriorating fiscal outlook, while the Bank of Japan kicked off a two-day policy meeting.

More on Japan economy:

data.title: Microsoft wins $170.4 million U.S. Air Force cloud contract
data.description:
• Microsoft (MSFT) has been awarded a $170.4 million contract to provide cloud services to the U.S. Air Force, the U.S. Department of War said in a statement.
  • Under the deal, Microsoft will supply Azure cloud services for the Air Force’s Cloud One program, which supports a range of military users. The work will be performed at Microsoft (MSFT) facilities across the continental United States and is scheduled to run through Dec. 7, 2028.
  • The contract was awarded without competition.

data.title: Crypto custody firm BitGo raises $212.8M in U.S. IPO priced at $18 per share
data.description:
• Crypto custody firm BitGo (BTGO) priced its US IPO at $18 per share, above the originally marketed range of $15–$17, raising $212.8M.
  • The offering values the company at $2.08 billion on a fully diluted basis with Goldman Sachs (GS) and Citigroup (C) as lead underwriters.
  • For the first nine months of 2025, the company recorded net income of $8.1M on revenue of $10B, versus the previous year's net income of $5.1M on revenue of $1.9B.
  • The IPO arrives amid crypto market volatility following an October 2025 sell-off, yet benefits from President Trump's pro-crypto policies and rising institutional interest.

data.title: Aris Mining reports 22% jump in 2025 gold production, guides higher for 2026
data.description:
• Aris Mining (ARMN) on Wednesday reported full-year 2025 gold production of 256,503 ounces, up 22% from the prior year, and said output is expected to climb to 300,000–350,000 ounces in 2026, driven by the ongoing ramp-up at Segovia and the expansion of the Marmato operation.
  • The increase was driven by the expanded Segovia mill and above-guidance performance at Marmato. Fourth quarter production of 69,852 oz capped off a year of strong operational execution.
  • Aris Mining (ARMN) said H2 2025 production of 143,088 oz, up 26% over H1 2025, reflects the installation of Segovia's second ball mill in June 2025.
  • The company reported a cash balance of over $390 million at December 31, 2025, net of the $60 million cash used for the Q4 2025 acquisition of the remaining 49% interest in Soto Norte.
  • Looking ahead to 2026, the miner expects consolidated production of 300,000 to 350,000 oz, supported by continued ramp-up at Segovia and increasing production from Marmato from Q4 2026 onwards, where active mining in the wider porphyry areas is already underway, materially de-risking the project.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Kinder Morgan tops Q4 estimates; initiates FY26 outlook below forecast
